---
title: "2025 v 2026"
author: "Jacqueline Hersh, Ph.D. & Ryan L. Farmer, Ph.D."
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-float: true
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    theme: cosmo
    df-print: paged
    fig-width: 10
    fig-height: 6
editor: visual
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center"
)

# -----------------------------
# APTC Color System
# -----------------------------

# Core brand colors
aptc_navy  <- "#252b3b"
aptc_coral <- "#ff6544"
aptc_teal  <- "#31a8c0"
aptc_white <- "#ffffff"

# Extended categorical palette (9 colors, stable + on-brand)
aptc_palette <- c(
  aptc_coral,   # coral
  "#ff8f77",    # light coral
  "#e24f33",    # dark coral

  aptc_teal,    # teal
  "#5bc0d4",    # light teal
  "#1f7f92",    # dark teal

  aptc_navy,    # navy
  "#454d5f",    # light navy
  "#161b29"     # dark navy
)

# Binary palette (Yes / No, True / False, etc.)
aptc_binary <- c(
  aptc_teal,
  aptc_coral
)

# Sequential palette (low -> high)
aptc_sequential <- colorRampPalette(
  c(aptc_white, aptc_teal, aptc_navy)
)(100)

```

# Setup and Data Loading

## Load Libraries

```{r libraries}
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)
library(freqtables)
library(skimr)
library(scales)
library(gt)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(patchwork)
library(viridis)
```

## 2025 Load Data

```{r}
library(readxl)
dat25 <- read_excel("2025dat.xlsx", na = "-99", 
     skip = 1)


dat25 <- dat25 %>%
  mutate(
    across(where(is.character), str_trim)
  )

dat25_quant <- dat25 %>%
  transmute(
    GrossSalary = as.numeric(`What is your Total Gross Salary (this excludes any stipends or grant-related work):`),
    FTESalary = as.numeric(`What percentage of FTE (0-100%) is this salary based on? FTE 100% refers to a full-time employee or the equivalent of a full-time employee.`),
    ClinicRevenue = as.numeric(`What is the average total annual revenue for your clinic?`),
    ClinicClientsCount = as.numeric(`How many clients/patients does your clinic service in an average year?`),
    ClinicTotalTrainees = as.numeric(`How many total trainees (doing therapy and/or assessment) does your clinic have in an average year?`),
    HoursWorkClinic = as.numeric(`In a typical week, how many hours per week do you work in your role in the clinic?`),
    ClinicAge = as.numeric(`How many years has your clinic been in existence?`),
    PD_amount = as.numeric(`Do you receive any annual professional development funds from your institution (e.g., conference travel, funds for licensing fees or CEs)? - Yes (if yes, please specify amount in dollars): - Text`)
  )

```

## 2026 Load Data

```{r load-data}

library(readxl)
dat <- read_excel("dat.xlsx")
dat <- dat[-1, ]
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

dat26 <- dat %>%
  mutate(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ na_if(., -99)),
    across(where(is.character), ~ na_if(., "-99")),
    across(where(is.character), str_trim)
  )



# -----------------------------
# GLOBAL DATA CLEANING (FIXED)
# -----------------------------

dat26 <- dat26 %>%
  # 1. Replace -99 with NA in numeric columns
  mutate(
    across(
      where(is.numeric),
      ~ na_if(., -99)
    )
  ) %>%

  # 2. Replace "-99" with NA in character columns
  mutate(
    across(
      where(is.character),
      ~ na_if(., "-99")
    )
  ) %>%

  # 3. Remove question text accidentally stored as values
  mutate(
    across(
      where(is.character),
      ~ if_else(
        str_detect(
          .,
          regex(
            "(\\?$)|(^what is )|(^last )|(^during )|(^in the last )",
            ignore_case = TRUE
          )
        ),
        NA_character_,
        .
      )
    )
  ) %>%

  # 4. Trim whitespace
  mutate(
    across(where(is.character), str_trim)
  ) %>%

  # 5. Normalize binary variables
  mutate(
    across(
      matches("Binary$"),
      ~ case_when(
        str_detect(., regex("^yes$", ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "Yes",
        str_detect(., regex("^no$",  ignore_case = TRUE)) ~ "No",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )
    )
  ) %>%

  # 6. Preserve your explicit numeric coercions
  mutate(
    Progress = as.numeric(Progress),
    Duration = as.numeric(`Duration (in seconds)`),
    GrossSalary = as.numeric(GrossSalary),
    FTESalary = as.numeric(FTESalary),
    RoleDuration = as.numeric(RoleDuration),
    ClinicDuration = as.numeric(ClinicDuration),
    ClinicAge = as.numeric(ClinicAge),
    HoursWorkClinic = as.numeric(HoursWorkClinic),
    CoursesTaught = as.numeric(CoursesTaught),
    CourseCreditHours = as.numeric(CourseCreditHours),
    ClinicTotalTrainees = as.numeric(ClinicTotalTrainees),
    ClinicClientsCount = as.numeric(ClinicClientsCount),
    NumRooms = as.numeric(NumRooms),
    ClinSqFt = as.numeric(ClinSqFt),
    SupTraineeCount = as.numeric(SupTraineeCount),
    MaxTraineeCount = as.numeric(MaxTraineeCount),
    HoursSupTherapy = as.numeric(HoursSupTherapy),
    HoursSupAssmnt = as.numeric(HoursSupAssmnt),
    HoursSupSup = as.numeric(HoursSupSup),
    HoursWatching = as.numeric(HoursWatching),
    HoursReviewingDoc = as.numeric(HoursReviewingDoc),
    MaxTraineesTherapy = as.numeric(MaxTraineesTherapy),
    MaxTraineesAssmnt = as.numeric(MaxTraineesAssmnt),
    TxLength = as.numeric(TxLength),
    MaxTxCasesAllow = as.numeric(MaxTxCasesAllow),
    MaxAxCasesAllow = as.numeric(MaxAxCasesAllow),
    ClinicRevenue = as.numeric(ClinicRevenue),
    ClinicTxRevenue = as.numeric(ClinicTxRevenue),
    ClinicAxRevenue = as.numeric(ClinicAxRevenue),
    Burnout_1 = as.numeric(Burnout_1),
    Burnout_2 = as.numeric(Burnout_2),
    Burnout_3 = as.numeric(Burnout_3),
    Burnout_4 = as.numeric(Burnout_4),
    Burnout_5 = as.numeric(Burnout_5)
  )

dat26_quant <- dat26 %>%
  mutate(
    PD_amount = as.numeric(PDFunds_2_TEXT)
  ) %>%
  select(
    GrossSalary,
    FTESalary,
    ClinicRevenue,
    ClinicClientsCount,
    ClinicTotalTrainees,
    HoursWorkClinic,
    ClinicAge,
    PD_amount
  )


# Display sample info
cat("Total respondents:", nrow(dat), "\n")
cat("Number of variables:", ncol(dat), "\n")
```

## Helper Function

```{r}
summarize_quant <- function(df, year_label) {
  df %>%
    pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Variable", values_to = "Value") %>%
    filter(!is.na(Value)) %>%
    group_by(Variable) %>%
    summarise(
      Year   = year_label,
      N      = n(),
      Median = median(Value),
      Q1     = quantile(Value, 0.25),
      Q3     = quantile(Value, 0.75),
      Mean   = mean(Value),
      SD     = sd(Value),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

```

## Summary Stats

```{r}
summary_25 <- summarize_quant(dat25_quant, "2025")
summary_26 <- summarize_quant(dat26_quant %>% 
                                select(GrossSalary, FTESalary, ClinicRevenue,
                                       ClinicClientsCount, ClinicTotalTrainees,
                                       HoursWorkClinic, ClinicAge, PD_amount),
                              "2026")

summary_all <- bind_rows(summary_25, summary_26)

```

## Filter

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

vars_to_plot <- c(
  "ClinicClientsCount",
  "ClinicTotalTrainees",
  "ClinicRevenue",
  "GrossSalary",
  "HoursWorkClinic",
  "PD_amount"
)

plot_dat <- summary_all %>%
  filter(Variable %in% vars_to_plot) %>%
  mutate(
    Year = factor(Year),
    Variable = factor(Variable, levels = vars_to_plot)
  )

```

## Visualizations

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

vars_to_plot <- c(
  "ClinicClientsCount",
  "ClinicTotalTrainees",
  "ClinicRevenue",
  "GrossSalary",
  "HoursWorkClinic",
  "PD_amount"
)

plot_dat <- summary_all %>%
  filter(Variable %in% vars_to_plot) %>%
  mutate(
    Year = factor(Year),
    Variable = factor(Variable, levels = vars_to_plot)
  )

plot_dat <- plot_dat %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = recode(
      Variable,
      ClinicClientsCount  = "Annual Clients Served",
      ClinicTotalTrainees = "Total Trainees",
      ClinicRevenue       = "Annual Clinic Revenue",
      GrossSalary         = "Gross Salary",
      HoursWorkClinic     = "Hours Worked per Week",
      PD_amount           = "Professional Development Funds"
    )
  )

ggplot(plot_dat, aes(x = Year, y = Median)) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = Q1, ymax = Q3),
    width = 0.15,
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  facet_wrap(~ Variable, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Key Clinic Metrics Show Minimal Year-to-Year Change",
    subtitle = "Medians with interquartile ranges (Q1–Q3), 2025 vs. 2026",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    caption = "APTC Clinic Survey"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

```

## Test of Equivalence

```{r}
long_dat <- bind_rows(
  dat25_quant %>% mutate(Year = "2025"),
  dat26_quant %>%
    select(GrossSalary, FTESalary, ClinicRevenue,
           ClinicClientsCount, ClinicTotalTrainees,
           HoursWorkClinic, ClinicAge, PD_amount) %>%
    mutate(Year = "2026")
) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Year,
    names_to = "Variable",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Value))

library(TOSTER)

run_tost <- function(x25, x26, var_name, eq_sd = 0.20) {

  x25 <- na.omit(x25)
  x26 <- na.omit(x26)

  sd_pooled <- sqrt(
    ((length(x25) - 1) * var(x25) +
     (length(x26) - 1) * var(x26)) /
    (length(x25) + length(x26) - 2)
  )

  low_eq  <- -eq_sd * sd_pooled
  high_eq <-  eq_sd * sd_pooled

  tost <- TOSTtwo(
    m1 = mean(x25),
    m2 = mean(x26),
    sd1 = sd(x25),
    sd2 = sd(x26),
    n1 = length(x25),
    n2 = length(x26),
    low_eqbound  = low_eq,
    high_eqbound = high_eq,
    alpha = 0.05,
    plot = FALSE,
    verbose = FALSE
  )

  tibble(
    Variable        = var_name,
    N_2025          = length(x25),
    N_2026          = length(x26),
    Mean_2025       = mean(x25),
    Mean_2026       = mean(x26),
    Mean_Diff       = mean(x26) - mean(x25),
    Low_EQ_Bound    = low_eq,
    High_EQ_Bound   = high_eq,
    TOST_p_lower    = tost$TOST_p1,
    TOST_p_upper    = tost$TOST_p2,
    Equivalent      = tost$TOST_p1 < .05 & tost$TOST_p2 < .05
  )
}


key_vars <- c(
  "ClinicAge",
  "ClinicClientsCount",
  "ClinicRevenue",
  "ClinicTotalTrainees",
  "FTESalary",
  "GrossSalary",
  "HoursWorkClinic",
  "PD_amount"
)
run_tost_by_name <- function(var_name, dat25, dat26, eq_sd = 0.20) {

  x25 <- dat25[[var_name]]
  x26 <- dat26[[var_name]]

  run_tost(
    x25 = x25,
    x26 = x26,
    var_name = var_name,
    eq_sd = eq_sd
  )
}
library(purrr)

tost_results <- map_dfr(
  key_vars,
  run_tost_by_name,
  dat25 = dat25_quant,
  dat26 = dat26_quant,
  eq_sd = 0.20
)

tost_results

```
